text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie. Neil, have you ever ridden a horse?
zh: 我是Neil。我是Georgie。Neil,你骑过马吗?

text: Yes, I have. I can't say that I enjoyed it much.
zh: 是的,骑过。不过说实话,我并不太喜欢。

text: I was a bit scared, to be honest. How about you? I used to ride horses a lot, actually.
zh: 老实说,我当时有点害怕。你呢?其实我以前经常骑马。

text: My favourite thing was to ride a horse through the countryside.
zh: 我最喜欢的事就是骑着马穿越乡间。

text: I love horses, they're so majestic and lovely. The relationship between humans and horses has been close throughout history,
zh: 我喜欢马,它们既高贵又可爱。人类和马之间的关系在历史长河中一直很密切,

text: but recently this has gone beyond riding horses for fun or using them for work.
zh: 但最近这种关系已经超越了骑马取乐或让马干活的范畴。

text: Equine -assisted services, sometimes called horse therapy,
zh: 马匹辅助服务,有时也称为马疗法,

text: is a health trend where caring and interacting with horses is used to heal physical and mental illness.
zh: 是一种健康趋势,通过照顾和与马互动来治愈身体和心理疾病。

text: Here's Daciani Navanayaga, presenter of BBC World Service programme The Conversation, to explain.
zh: 下面请听BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》主持人Daciani Navanayaga的讲解。

text: Equine Assisted Services is now an umbrella term
zh: “马匹辅助服务”现在是一个统称,

text: which encompasses a whole range of treatments involving activities and interactions with horses to promote better human wellbeing.
zh: 涵盖了一系列通过与马互动来促进人类福祉的疗法。

text: Equine Assisted Services is an umbrella term
zh: “马匹辅助服务”是一个统称——

text: – a term for a large number of different things which all belong to a single common category.
zh: 也就是用来指代许多不同事物、但都属于同一大类的一个词。

text: So, horse therapy includes many activities – from feeling the horse's movement,
zh: 马疗法包括很多活动——从感受马匹的movement,

text: which can help children with muscle disorders like cerebral palsy to simply grooming the horse, which can help calm anxiety.
zh: 这能帮助患有脑瘫等肌肉障碍的儿童,到简单地为马梳毛,这能帮助缓解焦虑。

text: Grooming a horse means cleaning and brushing the horse's coat of hair.
zh: 为马梳毛(grooming)是指清洁和刷理马的毛发。

text: Various animals can be used in therapy, but horses are particularly suitable as they're very sensitive to human emotions.
zh: 多种动物都可以用于治疗,但马特别适合,因为它们对人类情绪非常敏感。

text: Being in contact with them affects the limbic area of the brain dealing with stress.
zh: 与它们接触会影响大脑中负责处理压力的边缘系统区域。

text: In this episode we'll hear more about the horses helping people work through mental, emotional and physical challenges.
zh: 在本期节目中,我们将进一步了解马如何帮助人们克服心理、情感和身体上的挑战。

text: And as usual, we'll learn some useful new vocabulary.
zh: 像往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新词汇。

text: And remember, you'll find all the words and phrases from this episode on our website,
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站上找到本期节目中的所有单词和短语,

text: bbclearningenglish .com. But now I have a question for you, Neil.
zh: bbclearningenglish.com。不过现在,我有个问题要问你,Neil。

text: We've heard some of the special qualities horses possess, but which of the following about horses is also true?
zh: 我们已经了解了马的一些特殊之处,那么下列关于马的说法,哪一个是对的?

text: Is it a horses always sleep standing up?
zh: 是A. 马总是站着睡觉?

text: B. Horses can make over 100 sounds. Or C.
zh: 还是B. 马能发出100多种声音?或者C.

text: Horses have almost 360 degree vision.
zh: 马的视野接近360度?

text: Well, I'm sure I've seen a horse lying down, so
zh: 嗯,我确实见过马躺着睡觉,

text: I don't know about its vision though, so I'm going to say B. A horse can make over 100 sounds.
zh: 不过我不太清楚它的视野,所以我选B,马能发出100多种声音。

text: OK, well we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好,我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Horse therapist Claudia Nicholson first felt the healing power of horses as a child.
zh: 马疗师Claudia Nicholson小时候就第一次感受到了马的治愈力量。

text: She now runs her own centre for equine -assisted learning in the south of England, where she spoke with BBC World Service programme The Conversation.
zh: 她现在在英格兰南部经营着自己的马匹辅助学习中心,并在BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》中接受了采访。

text: I would spend hours on end sitting in a stable with my pony.
zh: 我常常一连几个小时坐在马厩里陪伴我的小马。

text: I was going through puberty, my parents had separated, I had a lot of unanswered questions
zh: 那时我正值青春期,父母也分开了,心里有很多没有答案的问题,

text: and I would feel met and safe and held with the connection
zh: 而在与小马的联系中,我能感受到被理解、被安全地拥抱着的感觉。

text: of the pony. As a child, Claudia spent hours on end with her pony.
zh: 小时候,Claudia常常一连几个小时陪伴她的小马。

text: Phrases like days or hours on end mean days or hours doing something continuously without stopping.
zh: “days on end”或“hours on end”这样的短语,意思是连续不断地做某事,持续好几天或好几个小时。

text: Spending time with her pony helped Claudia through puberty,
zh: 陪伴小马的时光帮助Claudia度过了青春期,

text: the stage of life when a child grows into an adult because of changes in their body which allow them to have children.
zh: 也就是身体发生变化、使孩子发育成能够生育的成年人的那个人生阶段。

text: It was her teenage experiences that led Claudia to start her own horse therapy centre.
zh: 正是她青少年时期的经历,促使Claudia创办了自己的马疗中心。

text: She works a lot with teenage girls and children in care.
zh: 她的服务对象大多是青少年女孩和寄养儿童。

text: A child in care is a child who's being looked after by the local authorities because they cannot be cared for by their parents or family.
zh: “受照顾儿童”(child in care)是指因无法由父母或家人照顾,而由地方当局负责照料的孩子。

text: Here, Claudia talks more about her work with children and horses to BBC World Service's The Conversation.
zh: 在这里,Claudia向BBC世界服务的《The Conversation》进一步讲述了她与孩子和马匹相处的工作。

text: It would take maybe a few sessions for a relationship of trust to be formed.
zh: 大概需要几次疗程,才能建立起彼此信任的关系。

text: And it's quite extraordinary to see a child that's experienced a lot of trauma, especially these children in care.
zh: 看到一个经历过大量创伤的孩子,尤其是这些受照顾的儿童发生变化,真的非常令人惊叹。

text: They can be brushing the pony's body.
zh: 他们可能正在为小马刷毛,

text: They might want to have a cry, which they've been holding on to.
zh: 这时也许会想哭出来,那是他们一直压抑着的情绪。

text: The horse regulates them. Many of the children Claudia works with suffer trauma.
zh: 马能让他们的情绪得到调节。Claudia接触的许多孩子都曾遭受过创伤。

text: warmer. It takes time for them to build a bond of trust with their horse.
zh: 变得更温暖。他们需要时间才能与马建立起信任的纽带。

text: The phrase bond of trust is a strong feeling of reliance and confidence between two people. Horses really are special animals.
zh: “信任纽带”(bond of trust)是指两个人之间那种强烈的依赖和信任感。马真的是非常特别的动物。

text: They sense human feelings like fear or sadness through breath, heart rate and movement, and respond with gentleness and calm.
zh: 它们能通过呼吸、心率和动作,感知人类的恐惧或悲伤等情绪,并以温柔和平静作出回应。

text: This makes them perfect animal therapists. And that's not all, right Georgie?
zh: 这使它们成为完美的动物治疗师。而且不止如此,对吧,Georgie?

text: Let's find out the answer to your question. Yes, Neil.
zh: 我们来揭晓你那个问题的答案吧。是的,Neil。

text: Well, I asked you which sentence about horses was true,
zh: 我之前问你,关于马的哪句话是正确的,

text: and you said that horses can make over 100 sounds.
zh: 你回答说马能发出100多种声音。

text: Sadly, that was the wrong answer. The correct answer was C – horses have near 360
zh: 很遗憾,这个答案不对。正确答案是C——马的视野接近360度,

text: degree vision, which is surprising. Wow, that's amazing. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: 这真是令人惊讶。哇,太神奇了。好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,

text: starting with umbrella term, a term used to describe many different things which all belong to a single common category.
zh: 首先是“umbrella term”(统称),指用来描述许多不同事物、但都属于同一大类的一个词语。

text: degree. Grooming an animal, like a horse or a dog, means brushing and cleaning its coat of hair.
zh: 度。为动物(比如马或狗)梳毛(grooming),是指刷理和清洁它们的毛发。

text: If you do something for hours on end, you do it continuously without stopping.
zh: 如果你连续不断地做某事,不间断,就可以说“for hours on end”。

text: Puberty is the stage of life when a child grows into an adult because of changes in their body which allow them to have children.
zh: 青春期(puberty)是指身体发生变化,使孩子发育成能够生育的成年人的人生阶段。

text: A child who is in care is being looked after by the local authorities because they cannot be cared for by their parents.
zh: “受照顾的孩子”(a child in care)是指因无法由父母照顾,而由地方当局负责照料的孩子。

text: And finally, a bond of trust is a strong feeling of reliance and confidence between two people.
zh: 最后,“信任纽带”(bond of trust)是指两个人之间强烈的依赖和信任感。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you'll find a quiz and worksheet for this episode over on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的测验和练习。

text: See you there soon, but for now it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快在那里见,现在先说再见。
